strengths: Deep distributed-systems and real-time backend expertise: built low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O, caching, and high-performance real-time metadata stores at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic — directly matching the event-streaming and real-time inference demands of the role.

Current hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure experience at re:cinq, building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — practical, present-day fluency in the exact tooling layer this role architects.

Fintech and regulated-market exposure (RIDE Capital, reshaping investment practices in Germany; multi-country pricing optimization at Optilyz) combined with ~19 years of engineering experience, comfortably exceeding the 10-year requirement.
concerns: No formal executive or engineering-management titles on record; the resume shows no explicit evidence of scaling a team from 10 to 30+ or developing an internal technical-leadership layer.
Agentic-AI work is described as infrastructure and tooling contribution rather than demonstrated ownership of production multi-agent systems with financial consequences.
No stated experience with LLM selection/fine-tuning/evaluation methodology, governance of outsourced engineering partners, or board-level roadmap communication.

key_factors.factor: High-scale distributed-systems and real-time backend depth
Hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure and MCP tooling experience
Fintech domain exposure and engineering-culture leadership (TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, founder ownership)

response.justification: This candidate pairs rare, directly relevant technical foundations with current agentic-AI momentum. Nearly two decades of engineering span the exact pillars of this CTO mandate: hyper-scale, low-latency microservices and real-time data systems at Sociomantic; event-driven platform architecture with Kubernetes deployment as founder of GISCollective; regulated German fintech at RIDE Capital, where disciplined craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) were applied to investment products; and, most recently, hands-on construction of AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq. The founder track record demonstrates end-to-end ownership and product judgment, while a sustained history of workshops, courses, and mentoring shows the instinct to develop other engineers — the raw material for building a senior leadership layer. Read in the strongest defensible light, this is a technically credible, AI-current, fintech-experienced builder whose depth maps tightly onto the role's architecture requirements, with executive scope as the growth edge rather than a technical gap.
